title: Ministers to Discuss US Military Use of Prestwick Airport

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: Defence Secretary, James Healey, announced that ministers will convene with John Swinney to address the usage of Prestwick Airport by the US military. The meeting will focus on clarifying the nature of military operations conducted at the airport.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: Healey emphasized that Prestwick Airport is not being used for any strike operations against Iran. The discussion aims to ensure transparency surrounding military activities at the facility, which has raised public interest and concern.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – Ministers will meet Swinney on US military use of Prestwick Airport, says Healey
